title: Biblical Regions
description: Broad regional study zones used for biblical orientation and narrative geography.

features.name: Galilee
features.description: Northern highlands and the lake region central to Jesus' ministry.
features.caution: The region is broad and its exact edge is schematic.

features.name: Samaria
features.description: The central hill country between Galilee and Judea.
features.caution: The boundaries changed across periods and are simplified here.

features.name: Judea
features.description: The southern hill country around Jerusalem and Bethlehem.
features.caution: This is a broad study overlay, not a precise provincial map.

features.name: Decapolis
features.description: A loose eastern network of Greco-Roman cities referenced in the Gospels.
features.caution: The Decapolis was a network, not one fixed border, so this polygon is intentionally approximate.

features.name: Moab
features.description: The Transjordan region east of the Dead Sea associated with the Moabites.
features.caution: The location is broad and should be read as a geographic guide only.

features.name: Edom
features.description: The southern Transjordan and Negev-adjacent highlands associated with Edom.
features.caution: This is a broad reconstructed region rather than a precise ancient border.
